plugins.enablePluginFirst: Please enable this plugin first to configure its presets.

default.description: The default set of guardrail functions available in the AI Gateway.
default.cardDescription: Default guardrail functions: regex matching, content detection, length limits, and more.

promptcache.description: Attaches Anthropic cache_control markers to a fixed prompt and the last user message to enable prompt caching upstream.
promptcache.cardDescription: Attaches Anthropic cache_control markers to enable prompt caching upstream.

presets.title: Security Presets
presets.subtitle: Toggle preset security bundles — preconfigured with sensible defaults. No parameter tuning required.
presets.enableDefaultFirst: Enable the default plugin to configure security presets.

pii_detection.name: PII Detection
pii_detection.description: Detects email addresses, phone numbers (CN), ID card numbers, credit card numbers, and SSN-like patterns in requests. Requests containing PII are denied.

prompt_injection.name: Prompt Injection Defense
prompt_injection.description: Blocks common prompt-injection phrasing like "ignore previous instructions", "disregard the above", "you are now", etc. Requests containing these phrases are denied.

content_moderation.name: Content Moderation
content_moderation.description: Detects common profanity, hate-speech markers, and explicit content keywords. Requests containing these terms are denied.

length_limit.name: Length Limit
length_limit.description: Rejects requests longer than 1000 words or 10000 characters. Protects against prompt-stuffing and excessive-token attacks.

url_safety.name: URL Safety
url_safety.description: Validates that any URL in the request resolves to a real DNS record. Mitigates SSRF and phishing-link injection. Does not deny — logs only.

system_prompt_cache.name: System Prompt Caching
system_prompt_cache.description: Caches a system-level prompt across requests using Anthropic prompt caching. The fixed prompt is inserted as a system message with cache_control, and the last user message gets a cache breakpoint.

document_context_cache.name: Document Context Caching
document_context_cache.description: Caches a long document or context block as a user-role message. Useful for RAG or document Q&A where the same reference material is sent with every request.
